name: Ursula K. Le Guin
bio: American author of speculative fiction, literary fiction, and essays. Known for her Earthsea cycle and Hainish Cycle novels.

name: Agatha Christie
bio: English crime novelist widely regarded as the Queen of Mystery. Created Hercule Poirot and Miss Marple.

name: P. D. James
bio: English crime writer and life peer, creator of the detective Adam Dalgliesh and Cordelia Gray.

name: Ruth Rendell
bio: English author of psychological crime fiction, writing as Ruth Rendell and Barbara Vine. Creator of Inspector Wexford.

name: Richard Osman
bio: British television presenter and author of the Thursday Murder Club series of cosy crime novels set in a retirement community.

name: Barbara Pym
bio: English novelist known for her sharp, witty observations of English middle-class life and the Church of England.

name: Penelope Fitzgerald
bio: English novelist, poet, and biographer. Won the Booker Prize in 1979 for Offshore. Known for spare, precise prose.

name: Bonnie Garmus
bio: American author and copywriter. Her debut novel Lessons in Chemistry became an international bestseller and was adapted for television.

name: Susan Cooper
bio: British-American author of children's fantasy literature, best known for The Dark Is Rising sequence drawing on Arthurian legend.

name: Donna Tartt
bio: American novelist best known for The Secret History and The Goldfinch, winner of the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in 2014.

name: Hilary Mantel
bio: English author and two-time Booker Prize winner for Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, historical fiction about Thomas Cromwell.

name: Alan Garner
bio: English author of fantasy and children's literature drawing on the mythology and landscape of Cheshire. Author of The Owl Service.

name: Elizabeth George
bio: American author of British-set crime fiction featuring Detective Inspector Thomas Lynley and Barbara Havers of Scotland Yard.

name: Ann Cleeves
bio: English crime writer, creator of the Vera Stanhope and Jimmy Perez series. Winner of the Crime Writers' Association Diamond Dagger.

name: Barbara Kingsolver
bio: American novelist and essayist whose work often explores the relationship between humans and their environment. Pulitzer Prize winner for Demon Copperhead.

name: Kazuo Ishiguro
bio: British novelist of Japanese origin, Nobel Laureate in Literature 2017. Known for The Remains of the Day and Never Let Me Go.

name: Diana Athill
bio: British literary editor and author of memoirs and fiction. Her memoir Somewhere Towards the End won the Costa Biography Award.

name: John Le Carré
bio: English author of espionage novels featuring George Smiley. Known for Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy and The Spy Who Came in from the Cold.

name: T. Kingfisher
bio: Pen name of Ursula Vernon, American author of fantasy and horror fiction. Writes adult dark fantasy as T. Kingfisher and children's books as Ursula Vernon.
top_work: A Wizard's Guide to Defensive Baking

name: Mary Oliver
bio: American poet whose work celebrates the natural world of New England. Won the National Book Award and Pulitzer Prize for Poetry.
